description: AI Tic-Tac-Toe Game - Play against an LLM-powered AI opponent (DeepSeek-R1 via AWS Bedrock) with game history persistence via PostgreSQL and PostGraphile GraphQL

userStories.title: Create backend project scaffolding
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the basic NestJS project structure so I can start building the tictactoe API.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/tictactoe-api/package.json with NestJS 8.x dependencies (mirror apps/realty-ai-api/package.json but remove @aws-sdk/client-bedrock-runtime and embedding-related deps)
Include scripts: dev (nest start --watch), build (nest build), start, docker:up, docker:down, typeorm:migration:new, typeorm:migration:run, migration, seed, typecheck
Create apps/tictactoe-api/tsconfig.json matching realty-ai-api/tsconfig.json
Create apps/tictactoe-api/tsconfig.build.json for NestJS CLI
Create apps/tictactoe-api/nest-cli.json matching realty-ai-api/nest-cli.json
Create apps/tictactoe-api/.eslintrc.js with root: true for NestJS decorator support (same pattern as realty-ai-api)
Create apps/tictactoe-api/docker-compose.yml with postgres:16 image on port 54322, db name 'tictactoe', credentials postgres/docker, volume tictactoe_pgdata

Create apps/tictactoe-api/.env.local with PORT=3002, POSTGRES_HOST=localhost, POSTGRES_PORT=54322, POSTGRES_USER=postgres, POSTGRES_PASSWORD=docker, POSTGRES_DB=tictactoe, GRAPHQL_ENDPOINT=/graphql, GRAPHIQL_ENABLED=true
Create apps/tictactoe-api/.env.example matching .env.local structure
Add apps/tictactoe-api to root .prettierignore and .eslintrc.js ignorePatterns
pnpm install succeeds from monorepo root
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Mirror realty-ai-api scaffolding. Root eslint/prettier configs don't support decorators - must add to ignorePatterns. NestJS CLI requires tsconfig.build.json.

userStories.title: Create backend database configuration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need database configuration utilities so TypeORM and PostGraphile can connect to PostgreSQL.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/config/postgres.ts with getConfig(), getAdminConfig(), getClient() functions (mirror realty-ai-api/src/config/postgres.ts but default port 54322 and db 'tictactoe')
Create src/config/typeorm.ts with getTypeOrmConfig() and getTypeOrmModuleConfig() using SnakeNamingStrategy (mirror realty-ai-api/src/config/typeorm.ts, include dotenv loading)
Create src/config/graphile.ts with postgraphileConfigFactory() (identical to realty-ai-api/src/config/graphile.ts)
Create src/config/entities.ts exporting empty entities array (will be populated when entity is created)
Configuration reads from environment variables with correct defaults
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: TypeORM 0.3.x uses DataSource. typeorm.ts needs dotenv.config() with explicit path for CLI operations. Export default DataSource for CLI and getTypeOrmModuleConfig() for NestJS.

userStories.title: Create Game entity
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Game entity so TypeORM can manage the game table schema.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/modules/game/game.entity.ts with TypeORM entity decorators

Entity has columns: id (uuid PK via uuid_generate_v4), board_state (jsonb, default array of 9 nulls), status (varchar(20), default 'in_progress'), winner (varchar(1), nullable), moves (jsonb, default empty array), created_at (timestamp), updated_at (timestamp)
Export CellValue type ('X' | 'O' | null), GameMove interface ({position, player, moveNumber}), GameStatus type
Update src/config/entities.ts to include Game entity in the exports array
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow Property entity pattern from realty-ai-api/src/modules/property/property.entity.ts. Use @CreateDateColumn and @UpdateDateColumn for timestamps. JSONB columns with typed interfaces.

userStories.title: Create NestJS application bootstrap
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the main application entry point so the API can start and serve requests.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app.service.ts with getRoot() returning {message: 'TicTacToe API'} and getHealth() returning {status: 'ok'}
Create src/app.controller.ts with GET / and GET /health endpoints
Create src/app.module.ts importing TypeOrmModule.forRoot(getTypeOrmModuleConfig()) and PostGraphileModule.forRootAsync()
Create src/main.ts bootstrapping NestJS on port 3002 with dotenv loading from .env.local
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use dotenv.config() with path.join(__dirname, '../.env.local') in main.ts. PostGraphileModule uses postgraphile-nest package with forRootAsync() and factory function from graphile.ts.

userStories.title: Create database migration and seed data
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the database schema created and sample data seeded so PostGraphile can generate the GraphQL API.
acceptanceCriteria: Start Docker with pnpm docker:up successfully
Create uuid-ossp extension in the tictactoe database
Generate TypeORM migration from Game entity using pnpm typeorm:migration:new
Migration creates 'game' table with all columns matching entity definition
pnpm typeorm:migration:run executes successfully
Create src/cli/seed.ts that inserts 2 sample completed games (one x_wins, one draw) with realistic board_state and moves arrays
Create src/cli/migration.ts as cleanup utility placeholder
pnpm seed runs successfully and data appears in database
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: uuid-ossp extension needs superuser privileges. TypeORM migration:generate requires -d src/config/typeorm.ts flag. Seed script needs dotenv loading and --project tsconfig.json flag.

userStories.title: Verify PostGraphile GraphQL API
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to verify the auto-generated GraphQL API works correctly before building the frontend.
acceptanceCriteria: pnpm dev starts backend on port 3002 without errors
GraphiQL accessible at http://localhost:3002/graphql
allGames query returns seeded games with fields: id, boardState, status, winner, moves, createdAt (PostGraphile converts snake_case to camelCase)
createGame mutation creates a new game record with default board_state and status
updateGameById mutation can update boardState, status, winner, and moves fields
CORS is enabled for cross-origin requests
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PostGraphile auto-generates CRUD from tables. Uses connection pattern: allGames { nodes { ... } }. JSONB columns accept JSON directly through GraphQL. Verify exact mutation input types in GraphiQL.

userStories.title: Create frontend project scaffolding
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Next.js frontend app set up with Tailwind and build tooling.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/tictactoe/package.json with Next.js 14, React 18, Tailwind, LangChain, Apollo Client, shadcn/ui dependencies (mirror realty-ai/package.json, remove weather/promptfoo deps)
Dev script runs on port 2025
Create apps/tictactoe/tsconfig.json extending @boilertowns-example/tsconfig/web.json with @/* path alias
Create apps/tictactoe/next.config.js (copy from realty-ai)
Create apps/tictactoe/postcss.config.js (copy from realty-ai)
Create apps/tictactoe/tailwind.config.ts (copy from realty-ai)
Create apps/tictactoe/.env.local with AI_AWS_BEDROCK_* credentials and NEXT_PUBLIC_GRAPHQL_URL=http://localhost:3002/graphql
pnpm install succeeds from monorepo root
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Copy .env.local Bedrock credentials from realty-ai/.env.local. Dependencies needed: @langchain/aws, @langchain/core, @langchain/langgraph, @apollo/client, graphql, zod, tailwindcss, class-variance-authority, clsx, tailwind-merge, lucide-react, @radix-ui/react-scroll-area, @radix-ui/react-slot.

userStories.title: Create frontend base files and UI components
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the base layout, styles, and shared UI components so I can build the game interface.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/globals.css (copy from realty-ai/src/app/globals.css)
Create src/app/layout.tsx with metadata title 'AI Tic-Tac-Toe' and basic html/body wrapper with Tailwind classes
Create src/lib/utils.ts with cn() utility using clsx + tailwind-merge (copy from realty-ai)
Copy shadcn/ui components from realty-ai/src/components/ui/ to src/components/ui/: button.tsx, card.tsx, badge.tsx, scroll-area.tsx
pnpm dev starts on port 2025 without errors
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: These are self-contained shadcn/ui components with no project-specific logic. Direct copy from realty-ai.

userStories.title: Create game types and deterministic game logic
userStories.description: As a developer, I need TypeScript types and pure functions for tic-tac-toe rules enforced deterministically without the LLM.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/types/game.ts defining: CellValue ('X' | 'O' | null), Board (9-element tuple of CellValue), GameMove ({position: number, player: 'X'|'O', moveNumber: number}), GameStatus ('in_progress'|'x_wins'|'o_wins'|'draw'), GameState ({id, board, status, winner, moves, isAiThinking}), GameSummary ({id, status, winner, moveCount, createdAt})

Create src/lib/game/gameLogic.ts with pure functions: checkWinner(board) checks all 8 win lines, isBoardFull(board), getAvailablePositions(board), getGameStatus(board), getCurrentTurn(board) based on X/O count, isValidMove(board, position), applyMove(board, position, player) returns new board immutably, createEmptyBoard() returns 9 nulls, formatBoardForDisplay(board) returns text grid for LLM prompt
WIN_LINES constant defines all 8 winning combinations: [0,1,2], [3,4,5], [6,7,8], [0,3,6], [1,4,7], [2,5,8], [0,4,8], [2,4,6]
All functions are pure with no side effects
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Board is a flat 9-element array. Indices 0-8 map left-to-right, top-to-bottom. X always goes first. formatBoardForDisplay shows '.' for empty, 'X'/'O' for played cells in a 3x3 text grid.

userStories.title: Create LangGraph AI move pipeline
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a LangGraph pipeline that sends board state to AWS Bedrock and returns the AI's chosen move with validation and retries.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/lib/ai/llm.ts with ChatBedrockConverse instance (model: 'us.deepseek.r1-v1:0', temp 0.7, maxTokens 256, credentials from AI_AWS_BEDROCK_* env vars) - same pattern as realty-ai/src/lib/ai/llm.ts
Create src/lib/ai/movePrompt.ts with ChatPromptTemplate: system message tells AI it plays O and must respond with ONLY a number 0-8; user message shows board display and available positions
Create src/lib/ai/moveValidator.ts with: Zod schema validating position is integer 0-8 and in available positions; parseMoveResponse() strips <think>...</think> tags and extracts first digit 0-8
Create src/lib/ai/graphState.ts with Annotation.Root defining: board, available_positions, board_display, ai_move

Create src/lib/ai/nodes.ts with 3 node functions: inputValidationNode (validates board, computes available positions, formats display), moveGenerationNode (invokes LLM with up to 3 retries, includes error feedback on retry), moveValidationNode (final legality check)
Create src/lib/ai/moveGraph.ts compiling StateGraph: START -> inputValidation -> moveGeneration -> moveValidation -> END
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow patterns from realty-ai/src/lib/ai/emailGraph.ts (StateGraph), nodes.ts (retry logic with MAX_GENERATION_ATTEMPTS=3), graphState.ts (Annotation.Root). DeepSeek-R1 wraps reasoning in <think>...</think> tags that must be stripped before parsing. Response content may be string or array of content blocks.

userStories.title: Create AI move API route
userStories.description: As a user, when I make a move the frontend needs an API endpoint to request the AI's response.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/api/ai-move/route.ts handling POST requests
Accepts JSON body: { board: CellValue[] }
Validates board is a 9-element array with valid values (null, 'X', 'O')
Returns 400 if game is already over (status !== 'in_progress')
Returns 400 for malformed JSON
Invokes moveGraph.invoke({ board }) and returns { position: number, board: CellValue[] }
Double-checks returned move is valid before responding (defense in depth)
Returns 500 with error message if LLM invocation fails after retries
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow pattern from realty-ai/src/app/api/generate-email/route.ts. Import game logic functions for validation. Apply AI move to board before returning updated board in response.

userStories.title: Create GraphQL integration layer
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Apollo Client and GraphQL queries/mutations so the frontend can persist and fetch game data.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/lib/graphql/client.ts with Apollo Client pointing to NEXT_PUBLIC_GRAPHQL_URL (default http://localhost:3002/graphql), cache-and-network for watchQuery, network-only for query
Create src/lib/graphql/queries.ts with GET_ALL_GAMES (orderBy: CREATED_AT_DESC, fields: id, boardState, status, winner, moves, createdAt) and GET_GAME_BY_ID
Create src/lib/graphql/mutations.ts with CREATE_GAME and UPDATE_GAME mutations using PostGraphile's auto-generated input types
Create src/lib/graphql/transforms.ts with transformGameToSummary() converting GraphQL node to GameSummary type
Create src/lib/graphql/hooks.ts with useGameHistory() hook: fetches all games on mount, returns {games, loading, error, refetch}
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PostGraphile converts snake_case to camelCase in GraphQL (board_state -> boardState). Auto-generated types: CreateGameInput has game: {...} wrapper, UpdateGameByIdInput has id + gamePatch: {...}. Follow pattern from realty-ai/src/lib/graphql/. Apollo Client uses typed generics for TypeScript safety.

userStories.title: Create game board UI components
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a 3x3 tic-tac-toe board and click cells to place my X.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/components/Cell.tsx: clickable button (96x96px) showing X (primary color), O (destructive/red color), or empty; hover state on clickable empty cells; disabled when occupied, game over, or AI thinking
Create src/components/GameBoard.tsx: 3x3 grid of Cell components with visible borders; passes onCellClick(position) callback to cells

Create src/components/GameStatus.tsx: shows contextual messages - 'Your turn (X)', 'AI is thinking...' (with animate-pulse), 'You win!' (primary color), 'AI wins!' (destructive color), 'It is a draw!'
Create src/components/NewGameButton.tsx: outline button that calls onClick to reset game state; always visible
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Use cn() from lib/utils for conditional classNames. Import Button from components/ui/button. Cell uses Tailwind classes for sizing and colors. GameBoard uses CSS grid (grid-cols-3).

userStories.title: Create game history sidebar components
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a sidebar showing my past games with results.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/components/GameHistoryItem.tsx: displays date/time (short format), move count, and result Badge (Win=default/green, Loss=destructive/red, Draw=secondary/gray)

Create src/components/GameHistorySidebar.tsx: Card with CardHeader 'Game History', CardContent with ScrollArea (max height ~400px) listing GameHistoryItems; shows 'Loading...' while fetching and 'No games played yet.' when empty
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Import Card, CardHeader, CardTitle, CardContent from components/ui/card. Import Badge from components/ui/badge. Import ScrollArea from components/ui/scroll-area. Date formatting: toLocaleDateString with month: 'short', day: 'numeric', hour: '2-digit', minute: '2-digit'.

userStories.title: Create main game page
userStories.description: As a user, I want to play a complete game of tic-tac-toe against the AI with game history displayed alongside.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/page.tsx as 'use client' component managing GameState via useState
handleCellClick(position): applies X immediately, checks win/draw, if game continues calls POST /api/ai-move, applies O response, checks win/draw again
On game end (win or draw): saves game to backend via createGame GraphQL mutation, refetches game history
If save fails, game still completes normally (error logged, not shown to user)
handleNewGame(): resets board to empty, clears error state
Error message displayed if AI move request fails; board remains in valid state
Layout: 2-column grid - game area with status/board/button centered (md:col-span-2), history sidebar (col-span-1)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: play a full game to completion, verify history sidebar updates
userStories.notes: Import all components, gameLogic functions, useGameHistory hook, apolloClient, and mutations. Game status checks after BOTH human and AI moves (human could win before AI plays). isAiThinking flag prevents clicks during API call. Frontend is source of truth for in-progress game; backend only receives completed games.
